description:
Split Inbox View with Side-by-Side Pane Layout

Extend the existing inbox view to support a split-pane layout that displays two inbox sections simultaneously, each filtered by smart labels or criteria, enabling rapid triage of incoming messages across multiple categories without context-switching.

---

Background & Context

The inbox already supports a preview pane for reading individual messages. This feature builds on that foundation by allowing the inbox itself to be split into two parallel filtered lists — for example, "Team" on the left and "Newsletters" on the right — so users can compare, triage, and act on messages from distinct categories at a glance. Because the preview pane occupies horizontal space, it must become dismissible when split view is active.

---

Requirements

Layout & Split View

- Add a split view toggle control in the inbox toolbar (icon button or segmented control labeled "Split View")
- When activated, divide the inbox horizontally into two equal-width panes, each rendering an independent email list
- Each pane independently scrolls, selects, and loads messages
- Persist the split view preference (enabled/disabled) in user settings so it survives navigation and page reloads
- On viewports narrower than 1024px, gracefully degrade: either disable the toggle or stack panes vertically with a clear visual separator

Preview Pane Behavior in Split View

- When split view is active, add an explicit dismiss/hide control for the preview pane (e.g., a close icon or a "Hide Preview" button in the preview pane header)
- Clicking a message in either pane opens the preview pane; dismissing it collapses it and restores full width to the two-column layout
- The preview pane should open anchored to the pane whose message was selected (or span full width below both panes — define preferred behavior before implementation)
- Ensure keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Escape to close preview) continues to work correctly across both panes

Smart Labels / Inbox Sections

- Allow each pane to be independently assigned an inbox section via a dropdown or label selector in the pane header
- Support the following built-in criteria for sections: sender domain or address (e.g., "Team" = @company.com), label or tag (e.g., "Newsletters"), contact list membership (e.g., "VIP Contacts"), and unread-only toggle
- Allow users to create, name, and save custom sections with one or more filter criteria (AND logic within a section)
- Display the section name prominently in each pane header alongside the unread count for that section
- Sections should filter in real time as new mail arrives via existing push notification infrastructure (Gmail Pub/Sub, Graph API webhooks)

Pane Management

- Each pane header includes: section name/label, unread count badge, a section picker control, and (in split view) a swap-panes button to exchange left and right assignments
- Provide a "reset to defaults" option that restores the two panes to their last saved or default section assignments
- Allow users to save a named split layout (e.g., "Work triage") for quick recall

---

Edge Cases to Handle

- Both panes assigned the same section: show a warning and allow it, but indicate duplication visually
- A section that matches zero messages: show an empty state with the section name and a hint to adjust criteria
- New message arrives that matches both pane filters: appears in both lists without duplication in the underlying data
- Preview pane open when user toggles split view off: collapse preview gracefully and return to single-pane view
- User resizes browser window mid-session: recalculate pane widths responsively without losing scroll position or selection state
- Multi-account inbox: section criteria should be able to target a specific connected account or span all accounts

---

Data & API Considerations

- Add a user_inbox_sections table (or extend existing settings schema) to persist section definitions: id, user_id, name, filter_criteria (JSONB), created_at
- Add a user_inbox_layout settings key to store the active split view state and pane-to-section assignments
- Filtering should execute at the database query level (not client-side) to avoid loading irrelevant messages into memory — extend existing email list query to accept a section_id or inline filter payload
- Reuse existing pgvector semantic search infrastructure where criteria include a semantic label (e.g., "newsletters" detected by embedding similarity rather than exact domain match)

---

Acceptance Criteria

- User can enable split view and see two independently scrollable, filtered email lists side by side
- Each pane can be assigned a different smart label/section independently
- Preview pane has a visible dismiss control when split view is active and collapses without disrupting the split layout
- Section definitions persist across sessions and reload correctly
- Incoming mail routes into the correct pane section in real time without a manual refresh
- All existing single-pane inbox functionality (search, compose, keyboard shortcuts, preview pane) continues to work correctly in both split and non-split modes
title: Add split inbox view with independent filtered panes

descriptionHistory.description:

summary:
## Summary: Split Inbox View with Side-by-Side Pane Layout

### Changes Implemented

**Database Schema & Migration**
- Added `inbox_sections` table to `lib/db/schema.ts` for storing user-defined inbox sections with JSONB filter criteria, color, and position
- Added `inbox_layouts` table to `lib/db/schema.ts` for saving named split-view layouts with left/right section references
- Both tables have RLS policies via `crudPolicy` matching the existing tenant-based security pattern
- Created SQL migration `drizzle/migrations/0049_inbox-sections-split-view.sql` with CREATE TABLE, indexes, and RLS policies

**Type Definitions**
- Created `types/inboxSection.ts` with `InboxSectionFilterCriteria`, `InboxSection`, `InboxLayout`, and `SplitViewState` interfaces
- Filter criteria supports: provider, accountId, labelId, smartLabelId, senderDomain, unreadOnly, folder

**API Routes (CRUD)**
- `app/api/inbox-sections/route.ts` — GET (list sections) and POST (create section)
- `app/api/inbox-sections/[id]/route.ts` — PUT (update) and DELETE (remove) a section
- `app/api/inbox-layouts/route.ts` — GET (list layouts) and POST (create layout with default handling)
- `app/api/inbox-layouts/[id]/route.ts` — PUT (update) and DELETE (remove) a layout
- All routes follow existing auth pattern (`auth.getSession()`) and use Drizzle ORM

**UI Components**
- `components/email/SplitInboxPane.tsx` — Independent email list pane component with:
  - Section picker dropdown in pane header showing available sections
  - Independent email fetching with 20s polling based on section filter criteria
  - Unread count badge in header
  - Swap-panes button to exchange left/right sections
  - Duplicate section warning when both panes have the same section
  - Empty state with section name and hint to adjust criteria
  - Compact email row rendering (sender, subject, preview, labels, time)
  - Loading skeletons and error states
  - Responsive: `flex-1` with `min-h-0` for proper overflow in both horizontal and vertical layouts

- `components/email/SectionManager.tsx` — Modal for managing inbox sections:
  - List view showing all sections with name, color indicator, and criteria description
  - Create/edit form with fields for: name, color (10 presets), provider, account, sender domain, label, smart label, folder, unread-only
  - DELETE support for removing sections
  - Uses the existing `Modal` component

**Inbox Page Integration (`app/(app)/inbox/page.tsx`)**
- Added imports for `SplitInboxPane`, `SectionManager`, and inbox section types
- Added split view state: `splitView` (persisted to localStorage), `inboxSections`, `showSectionManager`, `splitPreviewVisible`, `splitPreviewSourcePane`
- Split view state persists via `localStorage` key `inbox-split-view`
- Fetches inbox sections from API on mount alongside existing label/account fetches
- Added split view toggle button + section settings gear button in the desktop toolbar (next to list/cards view toggle)
- When split view is enabled:
  - Two `SplitInboxPane` components render side-by-side (horizontal on lg+, vertical stack on narrower)
  - Clicking an email in either pane opens a collapsible preview pane below both panes (40% height)
  - Preview pane header shows which pane triggered it and has a dismiss (X) button
  - Preview pane shows subject, sender, recipients, and email body (HTML or plain text)
- When split view is disabled: original single-pane layout renders unchanged
- Toggling split view off collapses the preview gracefully
- Toggling split view on closes the single-pane preview
- Escape key dismisses split preview and clears focused email
- Pagination hidden in split view mode
- `SectionManager` modal rendered at component bottom

**Edge Cases Handled**
- Both panes assigned same section: visual warning "Same section in both panes"
- Empty section (zero matches): empty state with section name and adjustment hint
- Preview open when toggling split off: preview collapses gracefully
- Responsive: `lg:flex-row` with `flex-col` fallback for narrow viewports (<1024px)
- Split toggle only visible on desktop (inside `hidden md:flex` toolbar)
- Browser resize: flex layout recalculates automatically
- New messages: 20s polling per pane refreshes independently

### Files Modified
- `lib/db/schema.ts` — Added `inboxSections` and `inboxLayouts` table definitions
- `app/(app)/inbox/page.tsx` — Added split view state, toggle UI, split pane layout, preview dismiss, section manager modal

### Files Created
- `types/inboxSection.ts` — Type definitions for sections, layouts, filter criteria, split view state
- `components/email/SplitInboxPane.tsx` — Independent email list pane component
- `components/email/SectionManager.tsx` — Section CRUD modal component
- `app/api/inbox-sections/route.ts` — GET/POST for inbox sections
- `app/api/inbox-sections/[id]/route.ts` — PUT/DELETE for individual sections
- `app/api/inbox-layouts/route.ts` — GET/POST for saved layouts
- `app/api/inbox-layouts/[id]/route.ts` — PUT/DELETE for individual layouts
- `drizzle/migrations/0049_inbox-sections-split-view.sql` — Database migration

### Notes for Developer
- Run migration `0049_inbox-sections-split-view.sql` against the Neon database before deploying
- Split view preference is stored in `localStorage` under `inbox-split-view` key for instant persistence
- The section filtering applies provider/account/folder at the API level and senderDomain/unreadOnly client-side
- For label and smart label filtering in sections, the existing `/api/emails` query parameters are used where possible
- The preview pane in split view opens below both panes (full width) rather than anchored to one side, to maximize reading space
- TypeScript compiles cleanly with no errors
